item.title: Kimi: Threat or menace?
item.dc:creator: Anthony Ha
item.description: Chinese company Moonshot AI released a new version of its Kimi model this week, prompting concern about "full AI communism."

item.title: All the EVs that were discontinued or killed off in the U.S. this year
item.dc:creator: Kirsten Korosec
item.description: Th Honda Prologue will no longer be sold in the U.S., joining a growing list of EV models to exit the market this year.

item.title: Federal employees can download TikTok on their work phones again
item.dc:creator: Anthony Ha
item.description: The Department of Justice says that federal employees can now download TikTok on their government devices.

item.title: A 600-mile road trip (and data) proves EV charging doesn’t suck anymore
item.dc:creator: Tim De Chant
item.description: A recent road trip in an EV revealed just how much faster and more reliable DC Fast charging has become in the U.S.

item.title: Neil Rimer thinks the AI money is coming back out
item.dc:creator: Connie Loizos
item.description: Neil Rimer, the venture capitalist who co-founded Index Ventures, predicts the historic wealth AI is generating in Silicon Valley will have to be redistributed, voluntarily or involuntarily.

item.title: Applications close in 48 hours — here’s everything Australian founders need to know about Stripe x Startup Battlefield
item.dc:creator: Isabelle Johannessen
item.description: The window is almost shut. On August 19, eight startups will take the stage at Stripe Tour Sydney in front of investors, global press, and the Australian tech community. One startup walks away with automatic entry into TechCrunch Disrupt in San Francisco — no application, no further competition, a guaranteed spot on the world’s most […]

item.title: Vertu wants executives to pay $6,880 for an AI agent — here’s how it actually performs
item.dc:creator: Jagmeet Singh
item.description: From AI workflows to battery life and security, here's what it's really like to live with Vertu's luxury foldable every day.

item.title: Databricks hits $188B valuation, extending its run as AI’s favorite second act
item.dc:creator: Julie Bort
item.description: Databricks has remade its image into an AI company and has published research on the cost savings of open weight AI models for coding.

item.title: The Zoom hack that says, ‘Don’t record me’
item.dc:creator: Connie Loizos
item.description: If every meeting, watercooler conversation, and date gets transcribed and summarized, who's actually reading any of it?

item.title: Agility Robotics plants its flag in Tesla’s backyard
item.dc:creator: Tim Fernholz
item.description: Agility is opening a new training center for its Digit robots in Fremont, California.

item.title: AI-driven memory crunch jolts India’s smartphone market
item.dc:creator: Jagmeet Singh
item.description: India's smartphone slowdown highlights how the AI boom is reshaping consumer electronics, from pricing and demand to corporate strategy.

item.title: Apple and Google ordered to purge ‘nudify’ apps from App Stores
item.dc:creator: Lucas Ropek
item.description: In letters sent to Apple and Google, San Francisco City Attorney David Chiu said that both companies have long been aware that they are hosting apps in violation of state law.

item.title: Nuclear startup Valar Atomics in talks to raise new funding at $6B valuation
item.dc:creator: Marina Temkin
item.description: The potential deal highlights a growing trend of complex, multi-stage funding rounds that mask true entry prices.

item.title: I replaced my space heater and ceiling fan with one Dyson appliance
item.dc:creator: Aisha Malik
item.description: Designed for year-round comfort, the Dyson Hot+Cool HF1 combines quiet operation and simple controls with Dyson's signature bladeless design.

item.title: How Apple’s big lawsuit could disrupt OpenAI’s IPO plans
item.dc:creator: Theresa Loconsolo
item.description: Apple filed a trade secrets lawsuit against OpenAI last Friday, and it’s not messing around. The complaint alleges a pattern of misconduct reaching all the way up to OpenAI’s chief hardware officer and claims more than 400 former Apple employees now work at the company. OpenAI’s response so far has been carefully hedged, and the timing couldn’t be worse with the company reportedly eyeing an IPO […]

item.title: FBI arrests man accused of using Steam games to drain victims’ crypto wallets
item.dc:creator: Lorenzo Franceschi-Bicchierai
item.description: Prosecutors accused 21-year-old student Zyaire Wilkins of publishing on Steam several fake video games that contained malware, infecting thousands of victims, and stealing crypto from some of them.

item.title: Parents want safer phones for kids. These companies are answering the call.
item.dc:creator: Lauren Forristal
item.description: As parents look for alternatives to unrestricted smartphones, a growing number of companies are building phones designed specifically for kids, from feature-limited mobile devices to minimalist home phones.

item.title: Amazon fixing bug that billed some AWS customers billions of dollars
item.dc:creator: Zack Whittaker
item.description: Some Amazon customers logged on Friday to a surprise bill estimate claiming that they owed the tech and cloud giant billions in fees.

item.title: Patreon stops asking AI bots not to scrape — and starts blocking them
item.dc:creator: Sarah Perez
item.description: Patreon is strengthening its defenses against AI scraping by working with Cloudflare to block bots that train AI models on creators’ content without permission. The move marks a shift away from relying on websites using robots.txt alone to actively block unauthorized AI training.

item.title: reMarkable’s new Paper Pure is good. That’s why I wrote this review on it.
item.dc:creator: Ivan Mehta
item.description: Remarkable's Paper Pure replaces Remarkable 2, and it is quite good.
